comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented: fsck configured-vs-mounted branch-mismatch WARN + dir-mismatch hint (configured=precedence-resolved, mounted=worktree checked-out branch). New hermetic e2e tests: defaults regression, custom dir+branch init creating/mounting/gitignoring with a real write+SYNC round-trip (push from clone A -> mount in clone B -> ticket visible), and both fsck mismatch checks; all run under 'not integration'. Subagent review clean (threaded repo_root for exactness; tightened the hint assertion). During final full-suite verification, found+fixed a config-cache regression the epic's early load_config calls exposed (a config appearing where none was discovered was missed) — also fixes latent MCP staleness. Full suite: 2949 passed, only the pre-existing unrelated test_external_isolation (grounding) fails.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Add tests proving a fresh `rebar init` honors a custom `tracker.dir` and `tracker.branch`
end-to-end, plus a regression test that defaults reproduce today's behavior, and an `fsck`
WARN when the configured branch/dir does not match what is actually mounted.

- e2e/init test: configure custom dir + branch in `[tool.rebar]`, run `init`, assert the
  orphan branch is created under the custom name, the worktree/symlink uses the custom dir,
  it is gitignored, and create/list/show round-trip.
- regression: with no config, the branch is `tickets` and the dir is `.tickets-tracker`.
- `fsck` mismatch: configured-vs-mounted divergence emits a WARN (not a hard error).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] e2e test: custom dir + branch init creates + mounts + gitignores correctly and round-trips.
- [ ] Regression test pins default dir/branch behavior.
- [ ] `fsck` WARNs on configured-vs-mounted branch/dir mismatch.
- [ ] `pytest -m "not integration"` green.

## Files
- `tests/interfaces/` or `tests/unit/` (new init/config test module)
- `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (mismatch WARN)

## Depends on
- Child 3 and Child 4 (the call sites must be migrated first).

## Review refinements (plan-review gate)
- E6/F1 (conf 0.86): the custom-branch e2e must exercise a **write+sync path on a non-default
  branch** (create -> push/pull or reconcile dry-run -> read-back), not only create/list/show,
  so the migrated push/sync/reconciler/advisory-lock paths are actually verified end-to-end.
- E5 (conf 1.0): branch/`dir` validator **accept/reject unit tests are owned by child 14b8** (the
  config-level validators); this child does NOT duplicate them — it covers init/e2e/regression/fsck.
- E2/T9 (conf 1.0): pin the fsck mismatch mechanism — "configured" = the precedence-resolved
  `tracker.dir`/`tracker.branch`; "mounted" = the actual worktree/symlink + checked-out branch
  (`git worktree list` / symlink target). The WARN compares those two.
- COH (conf 1.0): the init e2e creates a real orphan branch + worktree, but the default suite
  runs `pytest -m "not integration"` — the test must run there via a tmp-repo fixture (or be
  explicitly integration-marked AND a non-integration variant added). State which.
- [ ] e2e exercises a non-default-branch write+sync round-trip (not just create/list/show).
- [ ] (Validator accept/reject unit tests are covered by child 14b8 — not duplicated here.)
- [ ] fsck "configured" (precedence-resolved) vs "mounted" (worktree/symlink) source-of-truth pinned.
- [ ] The init test runs under `pytest -m "not integration"` (tmp-repo fixture) — or is dual-tiered.
compiled_state.title: tests: custom dir+branch init e2e, defaults regression, fsck mismatch WARN
